title: sorbus aria subsp. graeca
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. graeca, also known as the Greek whitebeam, is a subspecies of the mountain ash tree, which is native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant's berries are rich in antioxidants and have been used to boost the immune system, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. In herbal preparations, the berries can be made into a tea or used to create a tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
